from_shape.name: np.zeros
from_shape.intent: Return a new array of given shape filled with zeros.

inputs.name: shape
inputs.meaning: Shape of the new array.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The desired data type for the array.
constraints: must be a valid numpy dtype

inputs.name: order
inputs.meaning: Memory layout order.

outputs.meaning: Array of zeros with the given shape.

preconditions: shape must be a valid integer or tuple of integers
dtype must be a valid numpy data type
order must be 'C' or 'F' if provided
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the new array
failureModes: TypeError if shape is not an integer or tuple
ValueError if shape contains negative values

from_shape.name: np.ones
from_shape.intent: Return a new array of given shape filled with ones.

inputs.name: shape
inputs.meaning: Shape of the new array.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The desired data type for the array.
constraints: must be a valid numpy dtype

outputs.meaning: Array of ones with the given shape.

preconditions: shape must be a valid integer or tuple of integers
dtype must be a valid numpy data type
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the new array
failureModes: TypeError if shape is not an integer or tuple
ValueError if shape contains negative values

examples.description: 2D array of ones with dtype int

from_shape.name: np.eye
from_shape.intent: Return a 2-D array with ones on the diagonal and zeros elsewhere.

inputs.name: N
inputs.meaning: Number of rows in the output.

inputs.name: M
inputs.meaning: Number of columns in the output.

inputs.name: k
inputs.meaning: Index of the diagonal.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The desired data type.

outputs.meaning: 2D array with ones on the diagonal.

preconditions: N must be a non-negative integer
M must be a non-negative integer if provided
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the new array
failureModes: TypeError if N or M are not integers
ValueError if N or M are negative

from_data.name: np.array
from_data.intent: Create a numpy array from a list or tuple.

inputs.name: object
inputs.meaning: The input array or scalar value.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The desired data type for the array.
constraints: must be a valid numpy dtype

inputs.name: copy
inputs.meaning: If true, the object is copied.

inputs.name: order
inputs.meaning: The desired memory layout order for the array. C - row major or F - column major.

inputs.name: subok
inputs.meaning: If true, subclasses are preserved.

inputs.name: ndmin
inputs.meaning: The minimum number of dimensions for the resulting array.

inputs.name: like
inputs.meaning: Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not NumPy arrays.
constraints: must be a valid numpy array

outputs.meaning: The created numpy array.

preconditions: object is a valid list or tuple.
if dtype is provided, it must be a valid numpy dtype.
if order is provided, it must be 'C' or 'F'.
if like is provided, it must be a valid numpy array.
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the new array
failureModes: TypeError if object is not a list or tuple
TypeError if dtype is invalid
ValueError if ndmin is negative

from_data.name: np.asarray
from_data.intent: Convert the input to an ndarray.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Input data in any form that can be converted to an ndarray.
constraints: can be list, tuple, or existing array

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: By default, the data-type is inferred from the input data.

inputs.name: order
inputs.meaning: Whether to use row-major or column-major memory layout.

outputs.meaning: Array interpretation of a. No copy is performed if the input is already an ndarray with matching dtype.

preconditions: a must be convertible to an array
if dtype is provided, conversion must be possible
sideEffects: May allocate memory if conversion or copy is needed
failureModes: TypeError if a cannot be converted to array
TypeError if dtype is invalid

examples.description: Convert float list with dtype int

ranges.name: np.arange
ranges.intent: Return evenly spaced values within a given interval.

inputs.name: start
inputs.meaning: Start of interval. The interval includes this value.

inputs.name: stop
inputs.meaning: End of interval. The interval does not include this value.

inputs.name: step
inputs.meaning: Spacing between values.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The type of the output array.

outputs.meaning: Array of evenly spaced values.

preconditions: step must not be zero
start, stop, and step must be numeric
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the output array
failureModes: TypeError if start, stop, or step are not numeric
ValueError if step is zero

ranges.name: np.linspace
ranges.intent: Return evenly spaced numbers over a specified interval.

inputs.name: start
inputs.meaning: The starting value of the sequence.

inputs.name: stop
inputs.meaning: The end value of the sequence.

inputs.name: num
inputs.meaning: Number of samples to generate.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.meaning: If True, stop is the last sample.

inputs.name: retstep
inputs.meaning: If True, return (samples, step).

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The type of the output array.

outputs.meaning: num equally spaced samples or tuple (samples, step) if retstep=True

preconditions: num must be non-negative
start and stop must be numeric
sideEffects: Allocates memory for the output array
failureModes: TypeError if num is not an integer
ValueError if num is negative

arithmetic.name: np.absolute
arithmetic.intent: Calculate absolute value of each element in the input array.

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input array or scalar value.

inputs.name: out
inputs.meaning: A location into which the result is stored.
constraints: must be same shape as x

outputs.meaning: The absolute value of the input array or scalar.

preconditions: x is a valid numpy array or a scalar.
if out is provided, it must be writable.
x must not contain NaN values.
x must be of a numeric data type.
failureModes: TypeError if x is not a numpy array or scalar
ValueError if out shape does not match x shape
RuntimeError if x contains NaN values

arithmetic.name: np.add

inputs.name: x1
inputs.meaning: The first input array.

inputs.name: x2
inputs.meaning: The second input array.

inputs.name: out
inputs.meaning: Output array.

outputs.meaning: The sum of x1 and x2, element-wise.

preconditions: x1 and x2 must be compatible for broadcasting
x1 and x2 must be numeric arrays or scalars
failureModes: ValueError if x1 and x2 cannot be broadcast together
TypeError if x1 or x2 are not numeric

arithmetic.name: np.multiply

inputs.name: x1
inputs.meaning: The first input array.

inputs.name: x2
inputs.meaning: The second input array.

outputs.meaning: The product of x1 and x2, element-wise.

preconditions: x1 and x2 must be broadcastable
failureModes: ValueError if arrays cannot be broadcast together

arithmetic.name: np.divide

inputs.name: x1
inputs.meaning: The dividend.

inputs.name: x2
inputs.meaning: The divisor.

outputs.meaning: The quotient x1/x2, element-wise.

preconditions: x2 must not contain zeros
x1 and x2 must be broadcastable
failureModes: RuntimeWarning: divide by zero encountered in divide

arithmetic.name: np.power
arithmetic.intent: First array elements raised to powers from second array, element-wise.

inputs.name: x1
inputs.meaning: The bases.

inputs.name: x2
inputs.meaning: The exponents.

outputs.meaning: The bases in x1 raised to the exponents in x2.

preconditions: x1 and x2 must be broadcastable
failureModes: ValueError if arrays cannot be broadcast

arithmetic.name: np.sqrt
arithmetic.intent: Return the non-negative square-root of an array, element-wise.

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input array.
constraints: must not contain negative values for real output

inputs.name: out
inputs.meaning: A location into which the result is stored.

outputs.meaning: The positive square-root of each element.

preconditions: x must contain only non-negative values for real output
sideEffects: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered for sqrt if input contains negative
failureModes: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in sqrt (produces NaN for negative)

trigonometric.name: np.sin

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input angle in radians.

inputs.name: out
inputs.meaning: Output array.

outputs.meaning: The sine of each element in x.

preconditions: x must be in radians

trigonometric.name: np.cos

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input angle in radians.

outputs.meaning: The cosine of each element.

preconditions: x must be in radians

trigonometric.name: np.arctan2
trigonometric.intent: Element-wise arc tangent of x1/x2 choosing the quadrant correctly.

inputs.name: x1
inputs.meaning: y-coordinates.

inputs.name: x2
inputs.meaning: x-coordinates.

outputs.meaning: Angle in radians in [-π, π].

preconditions: x1 and x2 must be broadcastable

explog.name: np.exp
explog.intent: Calculate the exponential of all elements in the input array.

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input array.

outputs.meaning: The exponential of each element.

preconditions: x must be numeric
sideEffects: May produce very large numbers or overflow warnings
failureModes: RuntimeWarning: overflow encountered in exp

explog.name: np.log

inputs.name: x
inputs.meaning: The input array.

outputs.meaning: The natural logarithm of each element.

preconditions: x must contain only positive values
sideEffects: RuntimeWarning: divide by zero and invalid value for log if input contains ≤ 0
failureModes: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in log (produces -inf or NaN)

basic.name: np.mean
basic.intent: Compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array containing numbers whose mean is desired.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis or axes along which to operate.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: Type to use in computing the mean.

outputs.meaning: If axis is None, the result is a scalar. Otherwise, an array with the same shape as a.

preconditions: a must be array-like with numeric values
failureModes: TypeError if a is not numeric

basic.name: np.std
basic.intent: Compute the standard deviation along the specified axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Calculate the standard deviation of the array elements.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis or axes along which the standard deviation is computed.

inputs.name: ddof
inputs.meaning: Means Delta Degrees of Freedom. Default is 0.

outputs.meaning: Standard deviation of the array elements.

preconditions: a must contain numeric values

basic.name: np.var
basic.intent: Compute the variance along the specified axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array containing numbers whose variance is desired.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis along which to compute variance.

inputs.name: ddof
inputs.meaning: Delta Degrees of Freedom.

outputs.meaning: The variance of the array elements.

preconditions: a must be numeric

searching.name: np.argmax
searching.intent: Returns the indices of the maximum values along an axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Input array.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.meaning: If None, the index is into the flattened array.

outputs.meaning: Array of indices into the array.

preconditions: a must be array-like
failureModes: ValueError if array is empty

sorting.name: np.sort
sorting.intent: Return a sorted copy of an array.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array to be sorted.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.meaning: Axis along which to sort.

inputs.name: kind
inputs.meaning: Sorting algorithm. Can be 'quicksort', 'mergesort', 'heapsort', 'stable'.

inputs.name: order
inputs.meaning: When a is a structured array, this argument specifies which fields to compare first.

outputs.meaning: Sorted array.

preconditions: a must be array-like
failureModes: TypeError if array contains incomparable types

extrema.name: np.min
extrema.intent: Return the minimum of an array or minimum along an axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array with numbers.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis or axes along which to operate.

outputs.meaning: Minimum value.

preconditions: a must be numeric
failureModes: ValueError if array is empty

extrema.name: np.max
extrema.intent: Return the maximum of an array or maximum along an axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array with numbers.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis or axes along which to operate.

outputs.meaning: Maximum value.

preconditions: a must be numeric
failureModes: ValueError if array is empty

basic.name: np.sum
basic.intent: Sum of array elements over a given axis.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Elements to sum.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.type: None or int or tuple of ints
inputs.meaning: Axis or axes along which a sum is performed.

inputs.name: dtype
inputs.meaning: The type of the returned array.

outputs.meaning: Sum of array elements.

preconditions: a must be numeric
failureModes: TypeError if a contains non-numeric values

basic.name: np.dot

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: First argument.

inputs.name: b
inputs.meaning: Second argument.

inputs.name: out
inputs.meaning: Output argument.

outputs.meaning: Returns the dot product of a and b.

preconditions: a and b shapes must be compatible for dot product
a and b must be numeric
failureModes: ValueError if arrays have incompatible shapes

reshape.name: np.reshape
reshape.intent: Gives a new shape to an array without changing its data.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Array to be reshaped.

inputs.name: newshape
inputs.meaning: The new shape should be compatible with the original shape.
constraints: product must equal total number of elements

inputs.name: order
inputs.meaning: Read the elements of a using this index order, and place the elements into the reshaped array using this index order.

outputs.meaning: Reshaped array with new shape.

preconditions: newshape must be compatible with total number of elements
one dimension can be -1 to infer size
failureModes: ValueError if newshape is incompatible with a.size

reshape.name: np.transpose
reshape.intent: Permute the dimensions of an array.

inputs.name: a
inputs.meaning: Input array.

inputs.name: axes
inputs.meaning: If None or absent, transpose reverses the order of axes.

outputs.meaning: Transposed array.

preconditions: a must be array-like
failureModes: ValueError if axes is invalid

concatenate.name: np.concatenate
concatenate.intent: Join a sequence of arrays along an existing axis.

inputs.name: arrays
inputs.meaning: The arrays must have the same shape, except in the dimension corresponding to axis.

inputs.name: axis
inputs.meaning: The axis along which the arrays will be joined.

outputs.meaning: The concatenated array.

preconditions: all arrays must have compatible shapes except in concatenation axis
failureModes: ValueError if shapes are not compatible
